title: America's Bipartisan Tax-Cutting Mania
content:
Political analysts often warn to beware of any policy that is overwhelmingly popular, as it usually means it has not been properly scrutinized. Currently, the United States is in the grip of a bipartisan tax-cutting mania that threatens the nation's long-term economic stability. While tax refunds from massive deficit spending packages are appealing to the public, the economic realities paint a much darker picture. America is presently running record-high budget deficits outside of recession or wartime, causing immense concern among fiscal watchdogs. Despite these alarming economic indicators, both political parties continue to propose extensive tax giveaways in an attempt to buy lasting voter popularity.

Unfortunately, history shows that such tax giveaways rarely translate into sustained political success or meaningful improvements in purchasing power. Eliminating large portions of the population from the tax base entirely sounds appealing but comes with significant democratic downsides. Paying taxes gives citizens a tangible reason to care about the efficiency and effectiveness of their government. Severing that crucial connection risks deepening America's already profound political dysfunction by leaving millions as mere recipients of state largesse. Ultimately, this aggressive war on taxes pushes the mature conversation regarding necessary fiscal choices even further out of reach.
